stimulus: Scholarly discussions of class in Jane Austen's novels often celebrate the small rebellions of the writer's heroines against the rigid expectations ______blank by Regency-era English society. Most of the novels end in marriage, with the heroines settling into the established class roles after briefly straining against them, but there are some notable exceptions.
stem: Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?
rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. "Imposed" most logically completes the text. The text describes "rigid expectations" that Regency society placed on individuals; "imposed by Regency-era English society" captures the idea that society compelled people to live by those expectations.

Choice B is incorrect. "Committed" means done (in the sense of an act) or pledged; it doesn't fit "expectations committed by society." Choice C is incorrect. "Illustrated" means depicted, but the text describes the source of the expectations, not a visual presentation. Choice D is incorrect. "Interjected" means inserted suddenly into speech, which does not match the broader social pressure described.
